message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Mr. Lyons Steakhouse (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 797 reviews; Tripadvisor 281 reviews; OpenTable ~1,888 diner reviews — aggregate roughly 2,500-3,000 across major platforms
- Sentiment: mostly positive but trending mixed
- Longevity: Building operated as Lyons English Grille from 1945; purchased by Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar) in 2014, renovated and reopened as Mr. Lyons in 2015 — approximately 11 years under current concept, stable F10 ownership
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life — Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse 2021","Palm Springs Life — Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse 2022","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award (top 10% of properties)","Operated under Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar); Lazar has a James Beard Foundation chef profile [UNVERIFIED whether Mr. Lyons itself carries a James Beard nomination]"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably on narrow steakhouse-specific queries ('steakhouse Palm Springs', 'best steakhouse Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs supper club'). Does NOT surface in broader regional queries ('best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'best fine dining Coachella Valley 2025') where agents return Mastro's, The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, The Edge, Eddie V's, Morgan's, Wally's Desert Turtle instead. Visibility is anchored to the Palm Springs Life editor pick and to platform-listing presence (Yelp/Tripadvisor/OpenTable) rather than to recent editorial coverage
- Site structured data: [UNVERIFIED] The owned domain mrlyonsf10.com returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch, blocking direct inspection of schema.org markup, hours object, or menu structure. Bot-blocking itself is an agent-readability negative signal — an agent crawler hitting the same 403 cannot extract hours, menu, or location data from the canonical source and must fall back to third-party aggregators
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the web search results, here's information about Mr. Lyons Steakhouse in Palm Springs from Yelp and other review platforms:

## Overview
Mr. Lyons offers a modern steak experience with local farms in an elegant dining salon with personalized tableside service. Originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, the building was purchased and renovated to reopen in 2015 as Mr. Lyons.

## Reviews Summary
The restaurant has received mixed to positive reviews. It was established in 2015 and has garnered significant attention. According to the search results:

- Users have praised service as "beyond attentive and amazing," with Mr. Lyons described as "probably my favorite Steakhouse."

- One reviewer described having "one of the best steaks I've had" with excellent cocktails and service, calling it "worth every penny."

- However, some reviews noted inconsistency, with one diner reporting "the food just wasn't very good" and "the quality of the steaks themselves were rather poor and they weren't cooked properly."

## Atmosphere & Features
The dining rooms feature an old-school, dimly lit, mid-century steakhouse vibe with velvet booths, and many guests praise the steaks, cocktails and attentive service. Classic cocktails are served in the lounge, and private dining rooms and patio seating are available.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best steakhouses in Palm Springs for 2025:

**Mr. Lyons** - Mr. Lyons received "Editors pick for best steakhouse" from Palm Springs Life in 2021 and 2022, and reviewers rave about the "juicy, flavorful" steaks and the "life-changing" ribeye with bone marrow butter.

**G's Prime Steakhouse** - G's Prime Steakhouse is a premier destination for USDA Prime steaks, boasting an on-site dry-aging facility and an extensive wine list with over 500 selections.

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Located in nearby Rancho Mirage, The Edge Steakhouse is renowned for its exquisite steaks and seafood, and offers a dining experience that combines "6-star dining in a beautiful venue" with breathtaking views.

**Mastro's Steakhouse** (Palm Desert) - As an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, Mastro's Palm Desert continues to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley.

**Melvyn's** - Located in the iconic Ingleside Estate, Melvyn's is a Palm Springs institution, delighting guests since the 1970s, and is where Hollywood celebrities and political figures have dined.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2025:

## Steakhouse Options

Morton's The Steakhouse in Palm Desert is a renowned name in fine dining, specializing in prime-aged beef with an upscale atmosphere and impeccable service. The Steakhouse is a recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award and winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007.

## French & European Cuisine

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list. Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a top choice for classic French cuisine in a romantic setting.

## Italian Dining

Castelli's Ristorante in Palm Desert offers a taste of Italy in a charming, old-world setting with Italian classics made with the finest ingredients and warm, inviting atmosphere. Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine and warm, welcoming atmosphere with traditional Italian dishes prepared with authentic flavors.

## Seafood & Premium Dining

Eddie V's Prime Seafood in Palm Desert is known for its top-quality seafood and elegant dining experience with fresh seafood dishes and prime steaks. Eddie V's Prime Seafood is a luxury dining destination known for seafood flown in from pristine waters and premium hand-carved steaks artistically prepared with seasonal options.

## Other Notable Fine Dining

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine with dishes inspired by the flavors of the American Southwest. Italica wins Best Fine Dining in Indio according to Palm Springs Life's 2025 awards.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mr. Lyons in Palm Springs:

## Restaurant Overview

Mr. Lyons is a modern take on the classic steakhouse with a menu that puts local small farms first, located at 233 E Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs. The restaurant is rated 3.9 out of 5 on Tripadvisor with 281 reviews and ranks among the top restaurants in Palm Springs.

## Travelers' Choice Award

Tripadvisor gives a Travelers' Choice award to accommodations, attractions and restaurants that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor. Based on the search results, Mr. Lyons appears to have this designation.

## Reviews Summary

The Tripadvisor reviews for Mr. Lyons are mixed:

**Positive Feedback:**
- Some diners praise great cuts of meat, cooked as ordered, and tasty side dishes
- The bar area is noted as excellent and comparable to bars in major cities

**Negative Feedback:**
- Some reviewers report that food and service are embarrassingly bad, with difficulties finding staff to take cocktail orders 15 minutes after being seated
- Some steaks were reported to be of poor quality and cooked improperly, with some being more rare than rare

The reviews indicate considerable variability in dining experiences at this establishment.

I found search results for Mr. Lyons in Palm Springs! Here's a summary of the OpenTable reviews and other feedback:

## Restaurant Overview

Mr. Lyons originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, and was purchased and renovated in 2014-2015 as Mr. Lyons, a homage to Mr. David Lyons. Mr. Lyons features steakhouse classics with a modern edge.

## OpenTable Reviews

Mr. Lyons offers a sophisticated dining experience with "outstanding service" and "exquisite food," with regulars raving about the "delicious prime rib" and "superb Beef Wellington." The ambiance is described as "warm and stylish," perfect for special occasions, though some note a higher price point, the overall experience is "worth it" for a memorable meal.

## Recognition

Mr. Lyons is an editors pick for best steakhouse according to "Palm Springs Life 2021 and 2022."

## Dining Experience

The restaurant is a classic, old-school steakhouse with a glamorous, retro vibe featuring velvet booths, jazz standards, dim lighting, and well-dressed guests, with many guests calling it romantic and sexy.

Links: [{"title":"Tara Lazar | James Beard Foundation","url":"https://www.jamesbeard.org/chef/tara-lazar"},{"title":"Tara Lazar Joins the Palm Desert Food and Wine Dinner at the James Beard House in NYC — THE F(10)WORD","url":"https://thefword.blog/posts/2019/9/26/james-beard-dinner-2019"},{"title":"F10 Creative - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/tag/f10-creative/"},{"title":"Show 378, June 20, 2020: Tara Lazar, Founder, Foundation 10 Creative, Palm Springs Part One - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/media/podcasts/socal-restaurant-show/show-378-june-20-2020-tara-lazar-founder-foundation-10-creative-palm-springs-part-one/"},{"title":"Interview: Tara Lazar (Foundation 10 Creative, Palm Springs)","url":"https://foodgps.com/interview-chef-tara-lazar-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Tara Lazar — Snacky Tunes","url":"https://www.snackytunes.space/cmm/2018/7/16/tara-lazar"},{"title":"Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven: Where to Toast and Dine: Mister Lyons and Seymour’s Review by Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven","url":"http://pointeviven.blogspot.com/2017/07/Where-to-Toast-and-Dine-Mister-Lyons-Mr-David-Lyons-Seymours-Review-Jesse-Bluma-Pointe-Viven-Zomato-Number-1-One-Blog-Tara-Lazar-Foundation-10.html"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/tag/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"The James Beard Foundation Inc - Nonprofit Explorer - ProPublica","url":"https://projects.propublica.org/nonprofits/organizations/132752108"}]

Based on the search results, I found information connecting all the elements of your query:

Tara Lazar is the Founder of Foundation 10 Creative, a prominent, and growing, hospitality group in Palm Springs. Under the F10 family is the locals' favorite breakfast, lunch and brunch hotspot Cheeky's, the handsomely appointed modern steakhouse Mr. Lyons and their classic cocktail hideaway Seymour's, a lively and al fresco Italian eatery Birba and the sleek and serene 34-room boutique hotel, Alcazar.

Regarding the James Beard connection, F10 Creative founder Tara Lazar has been invited to join chefs Andrew Copley, Andie Hubka, Kenneth Williams and Gabriel Woo at the Palm Desert Food and Wine Dinner at the Beard House in New York City.

Links: [{"title":"161 Best Date Night Restaurants in Palm Springs, Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-date-night-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Romantic Restaurants in Palm Springs","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-zfp3-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Where to Go for Date Night in the Coachella Valley | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/where-to-go-for-date-night-in-the-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"Date Night, Upgraded: Palm Springs’ Most Romantic Speakeasy Bars | Blog — The Palm Springs Guys","url":"https://www.thepalmspringsguys.com/blog/date-night-upgraded-palm-springs-most-romantic-speakeasy-bars"},{"title":"Diners' Choice: Romantic restaurants in Palm Springs / Palm Desert","url":"https://www.opentable.com/s/dinerschoice?metroid=81&regionids=82&topic=Romance"},{"title":"2025 Best Romantic Restaurants Palm Springs | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/best-palmsprings-ca-top10-romanticrestaurant_35ps.html"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Romantic Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Romantic+Restaurant&find_loc=Palm+Springs%2C+CA"},{"title":"Fun and Romantic Date Ideas in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/fun-and-romantic-date-ideas-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Date Night Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA - Updated 2025 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=date+night+restaurant&find_loc=Palm+Springs,+CA"}]

Based on the search results, here are some excellent options for a romantic date night in Palm Springs with supper club and steakhouse options:

## Steakhouse Options

Mr. Lyons puts the sizzle in the steakhouse experience with classic and contemporary offerings. You can enter through Mr. Lyons Steakhouse and discover Seymour's, a hidden gem that exudes mid-century elegance with cozy leather banquettes and vintage artwork, plus an outdoor patio with a roaring fire pit.

There's also a downtown steakhouse with warm lighting, cozy booths, and a patio where steaks are seared to perfection and paired with an impressive wine list.

## Supper Club & Rat Pack Vibes

Palm Springs caters to those looking for a romantic supper club reminiscent of the Rat Pack era, a sophisticated steakhouse, or a cozy bistro with award-winning wine selections. Located on the premises of the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel, Melvyn's is elegantly upbeat and the home of authentic Hollywood glamour, offering martinis and live music for a sophisticated night out.

## Other Romantic Venues

The Pink Cabana within the famed Sands Hotel & Spa offers a one-of-a-kind atmosphere with a chic take on 1950s and 60s tennis and racquet clubs that feels like you're part of something secret.

Counter Reformation, tucked within the lush grounds of the Parker Palm Springs, offers a cozy setting reminiscent of a Parisian wine bar with an eclectic wine selection and savory small plates.

These options range from classic steakhouse experiences to vintage-inspired supper clubs perfect for a memorable romantic evening in Palm Springs.

LYONS, Palm Springs - Menu, Prices & Restaurant ...","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d7986645-Reviews-or15-Mr_Lyons-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Mr Lyons in Palm Springs, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Mr+Lyons&find_loc=Palm+Springs,+CA"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons - Review - Palm Springs - Palm Springs - The Infatuation","url":"https://www.theinfatuation.com/palm-springs/reviews/mr-lyons"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons FAQ (Palm Springs) - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/FAQ-g32847-d7986645-Mr_Lyons.html"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about Mr. Lyons on OpenTable with diner reviews:

## Restaurant Overview

Mr. Lyons offers a sophisticated dining experience with "outstanding service" and "exquisite food." Originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, in 2014 the building was purchased from David Lyons and after a complete renovation it reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons. The restaurant features steakhouse classics with a modern edge.

## Diner Reviews Summary

Reviews on OpenTable and other platforms show mixed experiences:

**Positive Reviews:**
- Regulars rave about the "delicious prime rib" and "superb Beef Wellington."
- The ambiance is described as "warm and stylish," perfect for special occasions.
- Multiple dishes were beautifully plated and delicious, specialty cocktails were fantastic, water glasses were never empty, finished plates were cleared quickly, and managers came to check in on diners, with everyone given VIP treatment.

**Critical Reviews:**
- Some diners found the prime rib "just okay — not bad, but not particularly memorable."
- Service complaints included slower than expected pacing, especially with food delivery.
- Some reported poor quality steaks that weren't cooked properly, with some being "more rare than rare."

Links: [{"title":"A little history - Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Steak House | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.mrlyonsf10.com/about"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse Reimagines Iconic 1940s Lyons English Grille in Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/mr-lyons-steakhouse-reimagines-iconic-1940s-lyons-english-grille-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/mr-lyons-palm-springs"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons : Palm Springs | Xtreme Foodies - The world's Essential Eats curated by local food experts","url":"https://www.xtremefoodies.com/north-america/palm-springs/review/Mr-Lyons/Mr-Lyons/9060_9336"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Palm Springs, California, United States | Venue Report","url":"https://www.venuereport.com/venue/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse – COACHELLA MAGAZINE","url":"https://coachellamagazine.com/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons - Steakhouse Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA | The Vendry","url":"https://thevendry.com/venue/110825/mr.-lyons-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"Palm Springs Weekend - Le Continental","url":"https://deanjab.com/2013/12/palm-springs-weekend/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/mr-lyons-steakhouse/58/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Lyons English Grille and its history in Palm Springs:

## History and Origins

Lyons English Grille originally opened in 1945, making it a historic establishment in Palm Springs. The restaurant was opened in 1945 by the Lyons family, and it was family-owned and operated by David Lyons for over 70 years.

## Design and Decor

The restaurant was known for its 19th century English décor dating back to the 1940s and became firmly established in Palm Springs restaurant history. The decor included coats of arms, heraldic flags, suits of armor, large stained glass portraits and scenes, art, Toby mugs, and much bric-à-brac, with much of the decor collected by David Lyons, who is originally from England.

## Legacy and Transformation

In 2014, the building was purchased from David Lyons and after a complete renovation, it reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons, a homage to Mr. David Lyons. Today, the restaurant continues as Mr. Lyons Steakhouse, honoring its historic heritage while offering a modernized dining experience.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best steakhouses in Palm Desert for 2025:

## Top Steakhouses in Palm Desert

**Mastro's Steakhouse** is a standout option. As an OpenTable Diners' Choice winner for 2025, Mastro's Palm Desert continues to be a top destination for unforgettable dining in the Coachella Valley. Mastro's offers nightly live entertainment to elevate your dining experience with top local musicians performing every evening. Mastro's maintains an upscale dress code with smart casual to business casual attire recommended, including dress shirts, cocktail dresses, jackets, and polished evening wear.

**Other Top Steakhouses:**

Best steakhouses in Palm Desert include Eddie V's Prime Seafood, Mastro's Steakhouse, Sullivan's Steakhouse, Ruth's Chris Steak House, LG's Prime Steakhouse, and Morton's The Steakhouse.

Sullivan's Steakhouse in Palm Desert offers a vibrant and lively ambiance with an exquisite seafood selection, featuring a seafood tower with lobster, tiger shrimp, oysters, ahi tuna, and jumbo Alaska crab legs.

Morton's The Steakhouse is celebrated for its "fabulous food and exceptional service," with USDA prime-aged steak and fresh seafood.

These establishments offer premium cuts, sophisticated atmospheres, and exceptional service for an upscale steakhouse dining experience in the Palm Desert area.

content.text: Confirmed: Cloudflare bot challenge (`cf-mitigated: challenge`) — the 403 is from Cloudflare's bot mitigation, blocking automated/agent fetches. This validates the "bot-blocking is an agent-readability negative signal" claim. Cannot independently verify structured data presence/absence.

confirms: Yelp shows 797 reviews (Yelp listing title confirms '797 Reviews')
Tripadvisor shows 281 reviews and Mr. Lyons holds the Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice designation (top 10%)
Building operated as Lyons English Grille from 1945; purchased from David Lyons in 2014, renovated and reopened as Mr. Lyons in 2015 under Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar)
Palm Springs Life Editors' Pick Best Steakhouse 2021 and 2022 — confirmed via multiple secondary citations referencing the awards
Tara Lazar has a James Beard Foundation chef profile (jamesbeard.org/chef/tara-lazar); no evidence Mr. Lyons itself carries a James Beard nomination

Sentiment is mixed-positive with notable trending-mixed signal: Yelp/Tripadvisor reviews show recurring complaints about inconsistency, undercooked/poor steaks, and slow service alongside positive raves

Visibility surfaces reliably on narrow steakhouse-specific Palm Springs queries (appears in Palm Springs Life steak roundup, OpenTable steak lists, supper-club/date-night roundups citing Seymour's hidden bar)

Does NOT surface in broad Coachella Valley 'best restaurants / fine dining 2025' queries — those return Morton's, Eddie V's, Mastro's, Morgan's, Le Vallauris, Chez Pierre, Castelli's, Italica instead of Mr. Lyons
mrlyonsf10.com returns HTTP 403 to automated fetches — Cloudflare bot-mitigation challenge confirmed via response headers (cf-mitigated: challenge), validating the agent-readability negative signal
Approximately 11 years under current Mr. Lyons concept (2015 reopen) with stable F10 ownership
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable diner review count of ~1,888 — opentable.com page would not load (timeout/dynamic JS), cannot independently verify the specific 1,888 figure; the page exists and reviews are extensive but the exact count is unconfirmed

Aggregate of 2,500-3,000 reviews across major platforms — dependent on the unverified OpenTable count; the 797 + 281 floor (Yelp + Tripadvisor) is ~1,078, so the aggregate claim hinges on the OpenTable figure

Structured-data assessment of mrlyonsf10.com — Cloudflare bot-block prevented direct HTML inspection of JSON-LD / schema.org / hours / menu markup; cannot confirm or refute presence of structured data, only that the canonical site is unreadable by automated agents (which is itself the agent-visibility finding)
Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier — these are evaluative ratings within the report's own framework, not externally verifiable facts
input.notes: Core visibility thesis holds up strongly. Independent queries confirm the asymmetry: Mr. Lyons surfaces consistently in steakhouse-specific and supper-club/date-night Palm Springs queries but is absent from broader Coachella Valley fine-dining lists (which favor Mastro's, Eddie V's, Morton's, Morgan's, Le Vallauris, Chez Pierre). Yelp 797 and Tripadvisor 281 review counts are exact matches. The 1945 origin, 2014 purchase, 2015 reopen, and F10/Lazar ownership chain are confirmed across multiple sources including Palm Springs Life. The 403 bot-block claim is verified at the HTTP-header level — Cloudflare returns cf-mitigated: challenge. Only the OpenTable-specific 1,888 figure and the resulting aggregate range could not be independently confirmed due to OpenTable's JS-heavy page failing WebFetch.
